articles.title: I Am Artemis: Jason Peterson
articles.summary:
Jason Peterson’s responsibilities for NASA’s Artemis II mission went beyond his usual role as the range operations manager at NASA’s Stennis Space Center near Bay St. Louis, Mississippi. 

Peterson credits his military experience with preparing him for the 12-to-16-hour workdays that helped bring the Artemis II test flight around the Moon and into view for audiences around the world.

articles.title: Open opportunities with ESA Navigation
articles.summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) leads the design, development, qualification and R&D of flagship EU Space programmes Galileo and EGNOS. It also implements satellite navigation programmes focused on innovation, competitiveness and in-orbit demonstrations, with ESA optional programmes NAVISP, FutureNAV, Celeste (LEO-PNT), Genesis and Moonlight.

articles.title: Hubble Details Early Galaxy Transforming Neighborhood
articles.summary: Astronomers using NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope have found something they never expected — ultraviolet light from a galaxy that existed just 1.4 billion years after the big bang.

articles.title: NASA’s Webb Pinpoints Millions of Stars Within Cigar Galaxy
articles.summary: Located 12 million light-years away and undergoing rapid star formation, edge-on spiral galaxy Messier 82 (M82) is a scientifically unique sight to behold, and now NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope has revealed previously unseen details. M82’s intense star formation, thought to be the result of a galaxy merger, will be a short-lived event in astronomical […]

articles.title: Loft Orbital to test AI models on spacecraft for Earth observation
articles.summary:
Loft Orbital is working with NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory to test the use of artificial intelligence on spacecraft to improve Earth science monitoring.

articles.title: Satellogic partners with SynMax to build intelligence services around upcoming Merlin constellation
articles.summary:

articles.title: China appears to be developing 7-meter-diameter reusable rockets
articles.summary:
A state-funded tender for tank tooling, a delivered stainless steel forging and launch pad planning suggest that China is developing 7-meter-diameter reusable rockets.

articles.title: Rising Waters Swamp Lake Naivasha
articles.summary: Relentless rains are threatening a lake in Kenya’s Great Rift Valley that has become a key hub in the global flower trade.
